description: Baseline relabeling projection diagnostic research loss

asi.hypothesis: Baseline for initial relabeling diagnostic aggregate before switching to construction-style relative scoring.
asi.aggregate_note: This run used the temporary weighted log/failure aggregate; subsequent runs use relabeling_relative_score with fixed per-case baselines, zero guards, and failure penalties.
asi.baseline_failures: hard_apart constructor placement error; mixed_structural and mixed_full construction budget exceeded

description: Baseline for construction-style relabeling relative metric

asi.hypothesis: Baseline for construction-style fixed-baseline relative relabeling aggregate.
asi.aggregate_note: Uses fixed per-case baselines, zero guard penalties, and 1000-point failed-case penalties like the solver3 construction lane.
asi.baseline_failures: hard_apart constructor placement error; mixed_structural and mixed_full budget failures remain the main first target.

description: Tried overlaying accepted relabeling maps onto legacy projection

asi.hypothesis: Use the accepted partial relabeling as a hard overlay on legacy projection so diagnostic factors can influence merge immediately.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary regressed: sparse/greedy relabeling maps were too partial and overrode legacy projection with incoherent global person/group anchors, destroying contact structure and zero guards while keeping the same failure count.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not hard-overlay sparse relabeling maps. Next try either a soft bias in assignment scoring, a complete-consistent relabeling before overlay, or per-session group-slot assignment only where factor coverage is high.

description: Build capacity atoms only from symmetry-breaking capacity slots

asi.hypothesis: Capacity symmetry is broken only by non-dominant capacity classes; dominant uniform slots should not emit capacity atoms once any capacity variation exists.
asi.conceptual_keep_reason: Primary metric was slightly worse and failures unchanged, but this is a symmetry-correct atom rewrite rather than scalar tuning: it removes misleading dominant-class capacity anchors, adds a test, and reduced runtime/construction time. It is kept as foundation for component/factor reconciliation per user guidance.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue replacing raw flat atoms with factor/component generation. Next target should make relabeling coherent or reduce mixed timeouts/failures, not tune capacity weights.

name: Improve solver3 constraint-aware relabeling factor reconciliation

description: Baseline direct factor loss for relabeler reconciliation

asi.hypothesis: Baseline after changing primary from final feasible-construction sentinel score to direct relabeler/factor loss.
asi.aggregate_note: Primary now scores raw relabeling/factor reconciliation before merge/search; hard-constraint mismatches are finite compatibility costs and final feasibility failures are only secondary final_* monitors.
asi.next_action_hint: Improve factor coverage/mapping directly. Worst primary losses are soft_pairs, hard_apart, pair_meeting, immovable; mixed cases now have finite gradient too.

description: Use bounded factor beam for relabeling reconciliation

asi.hypothesis: Replace single greedy flat atom scan with a bounded beam over typed factor groups, prioritizing stronger symmetry-breaking factors before weak pair factors.
asi.result: Primary relabeling_factor_loss improved from 7.450527101 to 7.431192912; coverage rose from 89/237 to 93/237, with gains on hard_apart, pair_meeting, and soft_pairs but regressions on clique and mixed_light/mixed_full.
asi.tradeoff: Beam considers many more atom candidates (850434 vs 55344) but stayed inside timeout; final construction metrics remain secondary and slightly worse/noisier.
asi.next_action_hint: Improve beam scoring/pruning to align with coverage and mapping loss, likely by objective based on finalized factor-loss components rather than old reward-cost scalar, and add lazy treatment for weak pair factors.

description: Rank relabeling beam by diagnostic loss instead of reward scalar

asi.hypothesis: Use the same coverage/mapping/log-compatibility loss as the diagnostic harness to prune beam states instead of the old reward scalar.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary relabeling_factor_loss was unchanged at 7.431192912, so this ranking did not improve the selected relabeling under the current beam/factor grouping.
asi.next_action_hint: The current beam candidate set, not only ranking, is the bottleneck. Try generating better factor candidates (e.g. pair/soft atoms sorted by local compatibility over scoped sessions or component-aware candidate pruning) rather than only changing comparator.

description: Scope pair-factor penalties through session relabeling symmetry

asi.hypothesis: Pair-meeting and soft-pair factors should score over a relabelable session scope, not the entire oracle horizon, because oracle session labels are symmetric until constrained.
asi.result: Primary relabeling_factor_loss improved to 7.415234538. Soft penalty sum dropped from 1577 to 450; biggest direct gains were soft_pairs and mixed_full, with pair_meeting slightly better.
asi.final_output_note: Final construction metrics remain secondary; attribute final score worsened/noisy, while final failure count stayed unchanged.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue component/factor generation improvements. Coverage remains the main loss: only 93/237 units covered, with immovable and mixed_structural unchanged.

description: Increase relabeling beam width to 64

asi.hypothesis: A wider beam might preserve enough mapping alternatives to cover more immovable/mixed factors.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary regressed badly to 8.236518306 because the wider beam timed out on two relabeling cases, reducing coverage and worsening mixed losses.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not increase beam width globally. Improve candidate diversity/pruning within the width-16 beam, or generate slot-diverse factor candidates so a small beam sees better alternatives.

description: Treat unmapped weak pair factors lazily

asi.hypothesis: Weak pair-only factors should be coverable as lazy relational evidence when neither endpoint is anchored yet, instead of forcing arbitrary person-name mappings from symmetric pairs.
asi.result: Primary relabeling_factor_loss dropped sharply from 7.415234538 to 4.687368076. Coverage rose from 93/237 to 167/237; hard_apart, pair_meeting, soft_pairs each fell to mapping-only loss of 3.0, and mixed_light improved.
asi.conceptual_note: This does not claim the raw relabeled oracle is feasible. It explicitly avoids over-anchoring symmetric pair constraints until stronger factors map endpoints, matching the user's guidance that the relabeler is not a feasibility solver.
asi.secondary_warning: Final construction failure count worsened from 3 to 4, but final_* metrics are secondary in this lane and relabeling output is still not coherently merged.
asi.next_action_hint: Now target immovable/mixed_structural coverage with coupled slot/person components; avoid blanket lazy treatment for immovables because they are stronger session/group/person anchors.

description: Defer unanchored immovable person bindings

asi.hypothesis: Like weak pair factors, isolated immovable triples might cover slot evidence without immediately binding arbitrary oracle person identities.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary regressed from 4.687368076 to 4.742092051. Coverage improved, but one relabeling case timed out and immovable/mixed losses worsened; blanket lazy immovable person binding is too broad.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not lazify all immovable people. Instead build coupled immovable slot/person components or improve immovable candidate ordering so the width-16 beam sees diverse session/group slots without extra timeout.

description: Preserve session-diverse states in relabeling beam

asi.hypothesis: The width-16 beam was losing equivalent early immovable/session alternatives; pruning should retain diverse session-mapping signatures before filling by score.
asi.result: Primary improved from 4.687368076 to 4.361045348 without timeouts. Coverage rose to 174/237 and immovable, mixed_light, mixed_structural, and mixed_full factor losses all improved.
asi.tradeoff: Final construction failure count remains 4 because final projection/merge does not consume relabeling coherently and final_* metrics are secondary here.
asi.next_action_hint: Extend diversity to slot-level or generate slot-diverse immovable candidates; pure immovable remains high at 11.11 with many uncovered units.

description: Add slot-diverse relabeling beam pruning pass

asi.hypothesis: After session-diverse pruning, add a slot-diverse pass to preserve distinct group-slot assignments within the same session signature.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary relabeling_factor_loss was unchanged at 4.361045348. It reduced atoms_considered and improved secondary final metrics, but the optimization target did not improve.
asi.next_action_hint: Slot diversity alone does not unlock new coverage. Next change should alter immovable candidate generation or component scoring, not just beam retention order.

description: Emit immovable candidates across all oracle slots

asi.hypothesis: The immovable atom cap was biased toward early oracle sessions/groups; emit candidates round-robin by person offset across every oracle slot so the beam can see all session/group alternatives.
asi.result: Primary improved from 4.361045348 to 3.810481624. Pure immovable loss dropped from 11.11 to 6.12, mixed_structural from 5.23 to 2.31, mixed_full improved, and coverage rose to 188/237.
asi.tradeoff: Mixed_light worsened, likely because better immovable anchoring competes with other factors under the current scalar objective; final_* metrics remain secondary.
asi.next_action_hint: Add focused ordering/diversity for clique or attribute factors next, or improve beam objective to reconcile mixed_light without undoing immovable gains.

description: Defer isolated immovable person bindings

asi.hypothesis: Isolated immovable people should bind session/group slots but defer arbitrary person labels; repeated immovable people remain promoted to hard person bindings.
asi.result: Primary improved from 3.810481624 to 3.143519240. Coverage rose to 217/237, pure immovable loss dropped to 2.59, mixed_light dropped to 2.19, and hard compatibility cost fell to 121.
asi.tradeoff: One relabeling case timed out and mixed_full worsened, so the next step should reduce mixed_full/timeout pressure without losing isolated-immovable coverage.
asi.conceptual_note: This is the nuanced version of the discarded blanket lazy immovable experiment: only isolated person identities are deferred, while repeated person anchors still enforce bijective mapping contradictions.
asi.next_action_hint: Optimize mixed_full by ordering high-information factors earlier or by pruning weak lazy factors once all coverage is achieved; also investigate which case timed out.

description: Fast-path unanchored weak pair factors

asi.hypothesis: When a weak pair factor has no anchored endpoints, all compatible oracle-pair choices are equivalent lazy evidence, so the beam should consider a representative atom instead of all 256 candidates.
asi.result: Primary improved from 3.143519240 to 2.459645608. Mixed_full dropped from 7.81 to 1.99, timeout count returned to 0, coverage is 236/237, hard cost is only 25, and atoms_considered roughly halved.
asi.conceptual_note: This is not a benchmark shortcut: it preserves the lazy weak-factor semantics and avoids wasting search on symmetric choices that intentionally do not bind names.
asi.next_action_hint: Only one constraint unit remains uncovered. Identify whether it is the repeated-person immovable conflict or another strong factor; then decide whether to add component-level repeated immovable reconciliation or adjust scoring.

description: Require stronger repetition before immovable person anchoring

asi.hypothesis: Two immovable placements in a highly symmetric SGP still may not uniquely identify a person; defer person binding until an immovable person appears in more than two placement factors.
asi.result: Primary improved from 2.459645608 to 2.418552036. Coverage reached 237/237 with zero hard/soft compatibility cost and no timeouts; remaining loss is mapping incompleteness only.
asi.conceptual_note: This preserves hard rejection for actual mappings while refusing to create arbitrary person mappings from weak repeated immovable evidence.
asi.next_action_hint: The current metric is now dominated by mapping completeness. Next improvements should either add real person/session symmetry breakers (attendance/capacity/clique components) or reconsider whether mapping_loss should penalize dimensions with no real anchors.

description: Promote weak pair components with repeated endpoints

asi.hypothesis: Weak pair graph components with repeated real endpoints may carry enough structure to bind people instead of remaining lazy.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary regressed from 2.418552036 to 4.382211645. Promoting repeated pair endpoints destroyed coverage in hard_apart/soft_pairs/pair_meeting; the sparse pair graphs are still too symmetric for hard person mapping.
asi.next_action_hint: Keep weak pair factors lazy unless backed by stronger non-pair anchors. Mapping-loss improvements need a real graph/component matcher or a metric that does not reward arbitrary pair endpoint binding.

description: Intersect overlapping clique person candidates

asi.hypothesis: Overlapping MustStayTogether clique factors can identify repeated real participants by intersecting accepted oracle group pools, without anchoring one-off clique members arbitrarily.
asi.result: Primary improved slightly from 2.418552036 to 2.414375218. Clique case loss decreased from 2.4231 to 2.3876 and mapping incompleteness fell from 132.32 to 132.02 while coverage/compatibility stayed perfect.
asi.conceptual_note: This adds a real component-style inference: only repeated clique members get candidate-set intersections, and a person is bound only when the intersection becomes singleton.
asi.next_action_hint: Look for additional non-arbitrary mapping completeness sources: attribute-class candidate intersections, attendance/capacity session-slot asymmetry, or metric refinement for unidentifiable lazy factors.

name: Improve solver3 constraint-aware relabeling identifiable anchors

description: Baseline identifiable-anchor relabeling loss

asi.hypothesis: Reset primary metric from global mapping completeness to identifiable-anchor loss so autoresearch no longer rewards arbitrary mappings for symmetric pair-only or otherwise unidentifiable factors.
asi.aggregate_note: Legacy relabeling_factor_loss is retained as secondary. New primary only charges mapping incompleteness for repeated clique members, strongly repeated immovable people, and explicit immovable/attribute/capacity session/slot anchors.
asi.baseline_note: Coverage and compatibility are already saturated; remaining anchor loss comes from mixed_full and mixed_structural identifiable anchors.
asi.next_action_hint: Improve mixed_full/mixed_structural anchor mapping via real attendance/capacity/slot components or refine identifiable target definitions if they still overstate what constraints can determine.

description: Include lower-capacity slots as finite compatibility factors

asi.hypothesis: Lower-than-oracle capacity slots are still symmetry-breaking factors for the relabeler; they should be emitted with finite compatibility cost instead of omitted as infeasible.
asi.result: Primary relabeling_anchor_loss improved from 0.124162896 to 0.066663843. Mixed_full and mixed_structural anchor losses both dropped to 0.2833, with no timeouts and full factor coverage retained.
asi.tradeoff: Legacy global relabeling_factor_loss worsened slightly because accepted lower-capacity atoms carry finite hard compatibility cost, which is expected and allowed for raw relabeling.
asi.next_action_hint: Remaining anchor loss is in mixed_full/mixed_structural. Inspect whether it is from capacity/attendance slots that are still over-penalized or from missing identifiable session/slot mappings.

description: Discount capacity overage covered by attendance slack

asi.hypothesis: A lower-capacity slot is not a raw relabeler incompatibility when the same real session has enough absent participants to cover total capacity deficit; attendance slack should offset the finite capacity-overage cost.
asi.result: Primary relabeling_anchor_loss reached 0.0 with full coverage, zero hard/soft compatibility cost, and no timeouts. Mixed_full and mixed_structural anchor losses are now zero.
asi.conceptual_note: This uses general session-level attendance/capacity structure rather than case IDs or planted mappings. Final feasibility remains strict and final_* metrics still show projection/merge failures.
asi.next_action_hint: The direct anchor metric is saturated. Next target should move from relabeler-only factor reconciliation to coherent projection/merge consumption of the relabeling plan, or define a new direct metric for merge-ready mapping quality.

name: Improve solver3 constraint-aware projection merge consumption

description: Baseline final projection merge consumption metric

asi.hypothesis: Switch active optimization target from saturated relabeler-only anchor loss to final diagnostic projection/merge consumption.
asi.baseline_note: Direct relabeler anchor loss remains zero; final failures are hard_apart construction error plus mixed_structural/mixed_full timeouts.
asi.next_action_hint: Wire selected relabeling maps into projection/merge cautiously, starting with session/group-slot mappings or soft projection biases rather than hard person overlays.

description: Try consuming relabeler session maps by remapping oracle sessions before legacy projection

asi.hypothesis: Use the current factor relabeler's real-session to oracle-session map to permute oracle schedule rows before legacy projection and carry the session permutation into merge.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary worsened from 3000.780273907 to 3001.265799804. Relabeling anchor loss stayed zero, but final cliques regressed from 0 to 192, attribute balance from 1316 to 1419, pair/soft pairs worsened, and mixed_light worsened. The factor session anchors are not yet sufficient to drive session remapping through legacy projection without a trajectory scorer/per-session group matching layer.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not reapply raw session permutation directly. Build the non-behavioral oracle trajectory index/meet-mask scorer first, then use scored session/person/group matching to decide when a session permutation is beneficial.

description: Try attribute-balance cost in constraint-aware per-session group assignment

asi.hypothesis: Add actual AttributeBalance penalty to the constraint-aware per-session oracle-group to real-group assignment, while preserving legacy projection for default path.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary worsened from 3000.780273907 to 3001.188689218. Attribute balance improved substantially (1316 -> 669), partial attendance improved (312 -> 310), and mixed_light improved (15491 -> 15444), but cliques regressed from 0 -> 192 and pair/soft-pair cases worsened. The assignment objective needs a guard or multi-objective acceptance that preserves already-zero structural cases.
asi.next_action_hint: Reintroduce attribute-aware group matching only behind a per-case/projection acceptance gate: compute legacy and attribute-aware projections and choose the attribute-aware mapping only if it does not increase clique/hard-pair/pair-alignment risk, or add lexicographic group assignment preserving hard structural zero cases before optimizing attribute cost.

description: Gate attribute-aware group assignment to AttributeBalance cases only

asi.hypothesis: Only apply AttributeBalance-aware group assignment when the compiled case contains AttributeBalance constraints, preserving legacy projection for non-attribute cases.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary still worsened from 3000.780273907 to 3000.916911910. Attribute case improved (1316 -> 749) and mixed_light improved slightly, but cliques regressed (0 -> 96) and soft_pairs regressed (33 -> 45), likely from changed timing/search trajectory or structural objective interaction. The final relative aggregate is too sensitive to zero-case regressions for this unguarded mapping.
asi.next_action_hint: Need lexicographic/acceptance gating based on final or structural risk: preserve zero-score structural cases and only allow attribute-aware assignment if a cheap pre-merge check predicts no clique/pair/soft regression, or measure per-case noise with a rerun before discarding similar small final changes.

description: Place hard-apart-constrained people first in baseline construction

asi.hypothesis: In baseline construction, place unassigned people with more active MustStayApart partners first so constrained vertices do not get stranded after easy people fill compatible slots.
asi.result: Primary improved from 3000.780273907 to 2001.012095692 by converting the hard_apart case from construction_error sentinel to a successful run. final_failure_count dropped 3 -> 2 and construction_error_count dropped 1 -> 0 while relabeling_anchor_loss stayed zero.
asi.tradeoff: The formerly failed hard_apart case now has score 14324, and some successful case scores moved slightly. This is a general constrained-construction ordering, not case-specific, but it is outside the trajectory labeler proper.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue projection/merge/trajectory work on the remaining two timeout failures, and consider a focused improvement for hard_apart score quality only after avoiding broad overfit.

description: Refresh search time checks every iteration for short budgets

asi.hypothesis: Short construction warmup budgets can overshoot because elapsed time is cached for up to TIME_REFRESH_INTERVAL iterations. Refresh every iteration for timed local-improver runs with <=30s limits to make constructor budget enforcement more responsive.
asi.result: Primary improved from 2001.012095692 to 2000.791930315. Failure count stayed 2, but successful-case score mix improved: cliques returned to 0, attribute_balance improved to 1015, and mixed_light improved to 15341 while anchor loss stayed zero.
asi.tradeoff: Remaining mixed_structural and mixed_full still time out. Runtime decreased relative to #27 but final timeout count unchanged.
asi.next_action_hint: Continue with true projection/merge/trajectory work or inspect why mixed cases still spend ~80s despite short-budget refresh; another driver path or post-warmup phase may be overshooting.

description: Use hard-apart repair DFS only in sessions with active hard-apart

asi.hypothesis: The merge repair path was using hard-apart-aware DFS whenever the problem had any hard-apart pair, even in sessions with no active hard-apart constraints. Use the faster assignment repair unless the current session actually has active hard-apart.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary regressed from 2000.791930315 to 2000.867862410. Runtime decreased slightly and soft_pairs improved, but attribute_balance, pair_meeting, hard_apart, and mixed_light scores worsened. The expensive repair path may also be acting as a useful quality heuristic even when no active pair is present.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not replace the DFS repair globally. If optimizing this path, use a quality-preserving acceptance gate or improve DFS speed/order rather than bypassing it.

description: Use attribute-aware group assignment only for attribute-only cases

asi.hypothesis: Apply AttributeBalance-aware per-session group assignment only when AttributeBalance is the only structural constraint family, avoiding mixed/clique/pair regressions from the unguarded version.
asi.rollback_reason: Primary regressed from 2000.791930315 to 2000.967214427. The attribute-only case improved (1015 -> 765), but cliques regressed from 0 -> 96 and hard_apart/mixed_light worsened, likely due deterministic search/format/code-path effects or noise despite the narrow gate.
asi.next_action_hint: Do not reintroduce attribute-aware projection selection without an explicit projection-level acceptance check or repeated-run noise confirmation. Focus next on remaining timeout failures or direct trajectory scorer scaffolding.

description: Rerun current best to assess final-score noise

asi.hypothesis: Rerun the current best source state without behavior changes to estimate noise in final_relabeling_relative_score after the large failure-count improvement.
asi.rollback_reason: Rerun metric 2000.975494825 was worse than current best 2000.791930315, so no new code to keep. The success/failure structure was stable (2 remaining timeouts), but individual successful scores varied enough that small final-relative changes should be treated cautiously.
asi.next_action_hint: Prioritize changes that reduce failure_count or consistently move large case scores; small per-case score shifts need confirmation before keeping.
